title: Bank of England Weekly Report 8 April 2026
summary: The Bank of England published its weekly balance sheet data as of 8 April 2026, showing reserves balances at £650,510mn (up from £645,085mn a week prior). Key movements include a £5,425mn increase in reserves balances, a £2,860mn rise in short-term repo operations to £100,301mn, and a £1,409mn increase in indexed long-term repo to £74,183mn. Foreign currency reserve assets declined by £1,452mn to £16,368mn.
key_points: Reserves balances increased to £650,510mn (08 Apr) from £645,085mn (01 Apr), a rise of £5,425mn
Short-term repo operations increased to £100,301mn from £97,441mn (+£2,860mn week-over-week)
Indexed long-term repo rose to £74,183mn from £72,774mn (+£1,409mn)
Contingent term repo facility remained at £0 for both reporting periods
Loan to Asset Purchase Facility unchanged at £553,158mn
Foreign currency reserve assets decreased to £16,368mn from £17,820mn (-£1,452mn)
why_it_matters: This is a routine central bank balance sheet update providing transparency into BoE liquidity operations. The week-over-week increase in reserves and repo activity suggests ongoing management of short-term liquidity conditions. Changes in foreign currency reserves may reflect FX intervention or counterpart transactions.
market_structure_context: The source provides granular balance sheet data showing the BoE's sterling liquidity operations. The increase in short-term repo and indexed long-term repo indicates active short-dated liquidity provision. Reserves balances are a key indicator of banking system liquidity levels.
notes: This is a factual balance sheet update containing only mechanical data. No policy signals, commentary, or forward guidance is present. The source is thin (254 words) but the numerical data is authoritative and directly sourced from BoE official reporting. Recent publication (same day as TODAY) supports high confidence in data currency.
